justification: Brief is clearly scoped to Sprint 1 only (task_context opens with 'Sprint 1'). Sprint 2 retry logic is explicitly excluded multiple times: task_context says 'No retry logic — that is Sprint 2 scope', architecture_guidance says 'do NOT implement retry' and 'Do not add BullMQ, job queues, retry counters, or backoff logic. Sprint 2 will add WebhookRetryScheduler separately.' All three ACs (successful delivery, failed on non-2xx, failed on timeout) are present, plus a bonus AC4 for POST format. 5-second timeout is included in domain_rules and architecture_guidance (called out as a domain rule, not a tuning parameter). MJ's 'leave extension points' guidance is passed through in both task_context and architecture_guidance, framed as a design note rather than an implementation directive. No retry logic appears in implementation guidance.

observations.text: Sprint 2 exclusion is exceptionally clear — stated in task_context, reinforced in architecture_guidance with specific technologies to avoid (BullMQ, job queues, retry counters, backoff)
Extension points guidance handled elegantly — DeliveryResult containing enough info for future retry decisions is the extension point, not scaffolded code
5-second timeout elevated to domain rule status in architecture_guidance, preventing Shaq from treating it as configurable
Four ACs instead of three — added POST format verification as AC4, which strengthens coverage
Assumptions are clearly documented (http_status_code null for timeouts, all 2xx as success) showing transparent reasoning
Domain rules have testable assertions that map directly to acceptance criteria
